description: You are a structural engineer designing a curved support bracket for a machine frame. Model a curved structural bracket in FreeCAD by sweeping a C-channel cross-section along a 90-degree circular arc. The C-channel profile should be sketched on the XY plane with 40 mm total height, 20 mm flange width, 3 mm web thickness, 3 mm flange thickness, and 2 mm internal fillet radius at all four web-flange junctions. The sweep path should be a quarter-circle arc of 150 mm centerline radius on the XZ plane, curving from the X-axis direction toward the Z-axis. After creating the swept solid, add mounting holes at both end faces: two 8 mm diameter holes per end face (one centered in each flange), each 15 mm deep, for a total of 4 holes. Save the model to ~/Documents/FreeCAD/curved_bracket.FCStd and export to ~/Documents/FreeCAD/curved_bracket.step.
